Container.Name: hardlink-organizer
Container.Overview: Preview-first web UI for planning and executing hardlinks across NAS storage pools. Scans ingress folders, previews destination paths before writing, validates same-device compatibility, and records full scan, link, and verification history. No files are moved or renamed.

Config.Name: Config Directory
Config.Description: Place config.toml here. DB and logs are stored here by default.

Config.Name: Media Disk 1
Config.Description: Root mount for your first media disk. Set host path to the disk root that contains your source and destination folders (e.g. /mnt/disk1). Both must live under the same disk root for hardlinks to work. Leave blank if unused.

Config.Name: Media Disk 2
Config.Description: Root mount for your second media disk. Same rules as Media Disk 1 — host path must match the disk root used in config.toml. Leave blank if unused.

Config.Name: Media Disk 3
Config.Description: Root mount for your third media disk. Leave blank if unused.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID to run the container process as. Run 'id -u' on your Unraid host to find your UID.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID to run the container process as. Run 'id -g' on your Unraid host to find your GID.
